title: rumex × subdubius
intro: Rumex × subdubius is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Rumex acetosella (sheep's sorrel) and Rumex arcticus (arctic dock). It is native to various regions, including Europe and Asia. Rumex × subdubius has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an antiseptic due to its high content of ascorbic acid and other bioactive compounds. The plant's leaves and roots can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, gastritis, and other gastrointestinal problems.
